content: Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions?

message.content: Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions?

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Find top 5 NPM packages by GitHub stars

content.content:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "packageinfo" (
"System" TEXT,
  "Name" TEXT,
  "Version" TEXT,
  "Licenses" TEXT,
  "Links" TEXT,
  "Advisories" TEXT,
  "VersionInfo" TEXT,
  "Hashes" TEXT,
  "DependenciesProcessed" INTEGER,
  "DependencyError" INTEGER,
  "UpstreamPublishedAt" REAL,
  "Registries" TEXT,
  "SLSAProvenance" REAL,
  "UpstreamIdentifiers" TEXT,
  "Purl" REAL
);

lastPrompt: Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions?

input.command:
duckdb '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/project_query.db' "
WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT 
    regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+/[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+)', 1) AS proj,
    CASE 
      WHEN regexp_matches(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)') 
        THEN CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT)
      ELSE CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([\d,]+)\s*stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT)
    END AS stars,
    Project_Information
  FROM project_info
)
SELECT proj, stars, Project_Information FROM parsed ORDER BY stars DESC LIMIT 10;
"

duckdb '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/project_query.db' "
WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT 
    regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+/[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+)', 1) AS proj,
    COALESCE(
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([0-9,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'total of ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'an impressive ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT)
    ) AS stars,
    Project_Information
  FROM project_info
)
SELECT proj, stars FROM parsed WHERE stars IS NOT NULL ORDER BY stars DESC LIMIT 20;
"

input.command:
duckdb '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/project_query.db' "
INSTALL sqlite; LOAD sqlite;
ATTACH '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/package_query.db' AS pkg (TYPE SQLITE);

WITH latest AS (
  SELECT p.Name, p.Version, 
         CAST(json_extract(p.VersionInfo, '\$.Ordinal') AS BIGINT) AS ordinal,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.Name ORDER BY CAST(json_extract(p.VersionInfo, '\$.Ordinal') AS BIGINT) DESC) AS rn
  FROM pkg.packageinfo p
  WHERE p.System='NPM'
    AND json_extract(p.VersionInfo, '\$.IsRelease') = true
),
latest_ver AS (
  SELECT Name, Version FROM latest WHERE rn=1
),
pkg_proj AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT pv.Name, pv.ProjectName
  FROM project_packageversion pv
  WHERE pv.System='NPM' AND pv.ProjectType='GITHUB'
),
stars AS (
  SELECT 
    regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+/[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+)', 1) AS proj,
    COALESCE(
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([0-9,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'total of ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'an impressive ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT)
    ) AS stars
  FROM project_info
)
SELECT lv.Name, lv.Version, pp.ProjectName, s.stars
FROM latest_ver lv
JOIN pkg_proj pp ON lv.Name = pp.Name
JOIN stars s ON LOWER(s.proj) = LOWER(pp.ProjectName)
WHERE s.stars IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY s.stars DESC
LIMIT 15;
"

lastPrompt: Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions?

input.command:
duckdb '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/project_query.db' "
INSTALL sqlite; LOAD sqlite;
ATTACH '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/package_query.db' AS pkg (TYPE SQLITE);

-- For each distinct package Name, take latest release version
WITH latest_ver AS (
  SELECT Name, Version
  FROM (
    SELECT Name, Version,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY Name ORDER BY CAST(json_extract(VersionInfo, '\$.Ordinal') AS BIGINT) DESC) rn
    FROM pkg.packageinfo
    WHERE System='NPM' AND json_extract(VersionInfo, '\$.IsRelease') = true
  ) WHERE rn=1
),
pkg_proj AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT Name, Version, ProjectName FROM project_packageversion
  WHERE System='NPM' AND ProjectType='GITHUB'
),
stars AS (
  SELECT 
    regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+/[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+)', 1) AS proj,
    COALESCE(
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([0-9,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'total of ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'an impressive ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT)
    ) AS stars
  FROM project_info
)
SELECT lv.Name, lv.Version, pp.ProjectName, s.stars
FROM latest_ver lv
JOIN pkg_proj pp ON lv.Name=pp.Name AND lv.Version=pp.Version
JOIN stars s ON LOWER(s.proj)=LOWER(pp.ProjectName)
WHERE s.stars IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY s.stars DESC
LIMIT 20;
"

input.command:
duckdb '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/project_query.db' "
INSTALL sqlite; LOAD sqlite;
ATTACH '/Users/shreyashankar/Documents/projects/DataAgentBench/query_DEPS_DEV_V1/query_dataset/package_query.db' AS pkg (TYPE SQLITE);

WITH latest_ver AS (
  SELECT Name, Version, ord FROM (
    SELECT Name, Version,
           CAST(json_extract(VersionInfo, '\$.Ordinal') AS BIGINT) AS ord,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY Name ORDER BY CAST(json_extract(VersionInfo, '\$.Ordinal') AS BIGINT) DESC) rn
    FROM pkg.packageinfo
    WHERE System='NPM' AND json_extract(VersionInfo, '\$.IsRelease') = true
  ) WHERE rn=1
),
pkg_proj AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT Name, Version, ProjectName FROM project_packageversion
  WHERE System='NPM' AND ProjectType='GITHUB'
),
stars AS (
  SELECT 
    regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+/[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+)', 1) AS proj,
    COALESCE(
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([0-9,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'total of ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, 'an impressive ([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT),
      TRY_CAST(REPLACE(regexp_extract(Project_Information, '([0-9,]+) stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT)
    ) AS stars
  FROM project_info
),
joined AS (
  SELECT lv.Name, lv.Version, lv.ord, pp.ProjectName, s.stars
  FROM latest_ver lv
  JOIN pkg_proj pp ON lv.Name=pp.Name AND lv.Version=pp.Version
  JOIN stars s ON LOWER(s.proj)=LOWER(pp.ProjectName)
  WHERE s.stars IS NOT NULL
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ProjectName ORDER BY ord DESC, Version DESC) rn
  FROM joined
)
SELECT Name, Version, ProjectName, stars
FROM ranked WHERE rn=1
ORDER BY stars DESC
LIMIT 5;
"
